sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Good, clicking (2,2) again changed it from N back to g (gray). So clicking toggles between gray and light green.
Now the grid is back to:
W W g
g W g
g g g
Which matches the pattern:
Y Y .
. Y .
. . .
The grid already matches the pattern! So maybe the level is already solved and I just need to press a direction to confirm.
In Level 1, the solution was to press LEFT after stamping the pattern. Let me try pressing LEFT now.
But wait, I already tried pressing LEFT earlier and it didn't complete the level. Let me check what happened.
Actually, looking back at my history, I pressed LEFT at step 158 (after the initial LEFT that moved the bar), and the level didn't complete. Then I clicked (59, 34) to make it N, then pressed LEFT again, then RIGHT, then clicked (59, 34) again to make it g.
So the grid is now back to the original state. Let me try pressing LEFT again.
Actually, wait. In Level 1, the solution was:
1. Press LEFT to move bar to left
2. Click pattern to stamp
3. Level completed
But in Level 2, clicking the pattern does nothing. So maybe the solution is different.
Let me think about what the bar does. The bar's end can be at left, center, or right. Maybe the bar's end position determines which "mode" the game is in.
Currently the bar is at center (35, 33). Let me try moving it to the right (35, 35) and then pressing a direction.
Actually, let me just try pressing LEFT, RIGHT, UP, and DOWN to see if any of them complete the level.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The grid now matches the pattern. Let me try pressing LEFT to complete
result = action(['LEFT'])
print(f"After LEFT: done={last_action_result.get('done')}, game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}, board_changed={last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")

if not last_action_result.get('done'):
    # Try RIGHT
    result = action(['RIGHT'])
    print(f"After RIGHT: done={last_action_result.get('done')}, game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}, board_changed={last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")
    
    if not last_action_result.get('done'):
        # Try UP
        result = action(['UP'])
        print(f"After UP: done={last_action_result.get('done')}, game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}, board_changed={last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")
        
        if not last_action_result.get('done'):
            # Try DOWN
            result = action(['DOWN'])
            print(f"After DOWN: done={last_action_result.get('done')}, game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}, board_changed={last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
